name: PASTA CAPONATA
description: This hearty dish features a rich Sicilian caponata sauce, complete with aubergine, nuts and raisins, which has superb full flavour, thick with an added celery crunch and a hit of flavour, plus more or less garlic (we like lots of garlic!) and then serve with pasta to soak up all that flavour.
method: Preheat oven to 180°C | Line a baking tray | Large frying pan with lid on a medium heat | Large saucepan | Small frying pan

Trim the aubergines and chop the flesh into 2cm cubes. Lay on the lined baking tray along with the cherry tomatoes and drizzle with 1 tablespoon of the olive oil. Sprinkle with a good layer of salt, pepper and the chili flakes, put the tray in the preheated oven and bake for 30 minutes

Meanwhile, pour the remaining 2 tablespoons of oil into the large frying pan. Peel and finely dice the onions and add them to the pan. Peel and dice the leaves and cook from the celery, then finely chop and add to the pan along with the onions. Add the oregano and cook, stirring regularly, for 8 minutes

Add the tomato puree to the pan and stir. Add the chopped tomatoes, capers and balsamic vinegar to the pan. Bring the mixture to a simmer, stir to taste, then reduce the heat to a gentle simmer and let everything cook for 5 minutes. Remove the roasted aubergines and tomatoes from the oven and add them to the pan, giving everything a stir. Put the lid on and simmer for 12-15 minutes, stirring every 5 minutes to stop it from sticking

Boil a kettle and fill the large saucepan with the boiling water and a pinch of salt. Add the pasta and cook until al dente, following the instructions on the packet. Strain away the water and tip the cooked pasta back into the pasta pan

Meanwhile, chop or grate the dark chocolate and sprinkle it into the caponata sauce. Chop the stalks from the parsley and save the sprinkles and add. Chop the leaves and add three-quarters to the pan along with the balsamic vinegar. Simmer for a further 2-5 minutes until the oil has separated (add water if necessary). Pour the sauce over the pasta and fold in, making sure everything is well covered
Put the small frying pan on a medium-high heat and toast the pine nuts in the dry pan until golden. Sprinkle over the pasta along with the reserved parsley leaves before serving
